---
title: "Spinoza Verification and Proof-Carrying Claims"
chapter_id: "spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-claims"
part_id: "planning-memory-reasoning-execution"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "spinoza"
  - "genesiscode"
  - "coherence_exchange"
  - "verification_bandwidth"
  - "treellm"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Spinoza Verification and Proof-Carrying Claims

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-claims` |
| Part | Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `spinoza`, `genesiscode`, `coherence_exchange`, `verification_bandwidth`, `treellm` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The stack needs a way to move selected claims from generated prose toward auditable, proof-carrying objects.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Neural generation alone cannot guarantee semantic consistency, source grounding, or formal validity.

## Core Claim

[spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-claims.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] Spinoza-style verification should convert high-value claims into proof-carrying or justification-carrying artifacts with explicit tiers.

## Mechanism

- Separate heuristic proposal from symbolic or structured interrogation.
- Attach formal interpretation mappings, proof bundles, or citation-backed dossiers.
- Downgrade or escalate claims that cannot meet their required tier.

## Interfaces

- Claim ledger stores proof status.
- GenesisCode and Lean provide formal hooks.
- UAT provides adversarial review.

## Invariants

- Proof tier is explicit.
- A justification artifact is required for promoted claims.
- Unsupported claims remain marked speculative or blocked.

## Failure modes

- Certified delusion.
- Invalid formalization.
- Justification artifact missing.

## Minimal implementation

A tiered proof-carrying claim schema with one non-philosophical invariant example.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Proof artifact presence test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Tier assignment test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Formalization mismatch review | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. |
| `genesiscode` | GenesisCode | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Tiny pure calculus + obligations + provenance for auditable AI-symbiotic programming. |
| `coherence_exchange` | The Coherence Exchange | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Found in AI generated paper dump. Use carefully; speculative synthesis of PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, UAT, Alignment Field. |
| `verification_bandwidth` | Verification Bandwidth in Bounded Contexts | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Generation vs verification; long context is not automatically long-range reasoning. |
| `treellm` | TreeLLM | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Hierarchical semantic token system for grounded, efficient, explainable language modeling. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
